lofi.description: Lo-fi hip hop and chill-hop. Slow, mellow beats with warm analog textures, often layered with ambient noise like rain or coffee-shop murmur. The genre of late-night studying, focused work, and quiet relaxation.

ambient.description: Atmospheric, texture-driven music with minimal rhythmic structure. Designed to blend into environments rather than demand attention. Works for deep focus, meditation, or sleep.

pop.description: Polished, melodically driven mainstream music with strong hooks and production. Spans a wide energy range from ballads to dancefloor anthems. Broadly accessible.

indie pop.description: Melodic, guitar-forward pop with an independent aesthetic. Warmer and more organic than mainstream pop, often introspective with strong hooks.

rock.description: Guitar-driven music with strong rhythm and dynamics. Ranges from mid-tempo alternative to high-energy hard rock. Common emotional territory: intensity, catharsis, empowerment.

metal.description: Extremely high-energy, aggressive guitar music with complex rhythms and powerful dynamics. The genre for extreme emotional catharsis, maximum intensity, and workout fuel.

jazz.description: Improvised or arrangement-based music with complex harmonic structures. Acoustic instruments dominate — piano, upright bass, horns, brushed drums. Warm, sophisticated, relaxed.

blues.description: Emotionally expressive acoustic and electric guitar music rooted in African-American tradition. Slow, melancholy, and cathartic. Common feelings: sorrow, longing, weariness.

soul.description: Warm, emotionally rich music rooted in gospel and R&B. Features powerful vocals, organic instrumentation, and themes of love, longing, and resilience.

r&b.description: Rhythm and blues: smooth, groove-heavy music blending soul, funk, and hip-hop influences. Often moody, sensual, and introspective. A good pick for late-night or reflective moods.

hip-hop.description: Beat-driven music with rhythmic vocal delivery. Ranges from introspective to high-energy. Strong sense of rhythm and pulse — good for focused work or moderate-energy activities.

edm.description: Electronic Dance Music: synthesizer-driven, maximally energetic, built for dancefloors. Very high energy, heavy bass drops, repetitive rhythmic structures that build tension and release.

synthwave.description: Retro-futuristic electronic music inspired by 1980s film soundtracks. Pulsing synth arpeggios, neon aesthetics, nocturnal mood. Good for late-night drives or atmospheric work.

classical.description: Western orchestral music from the baroque through romantic periods, or contemporary compositions in that tradition. Highly acoustic, emotionally wide-ranging. Associated with focus, introspection, and melancholy.

folk.description: Acoustic storytelling music rooted in oral tradition. Acoustic guitar, light percussion, intimate vocals. Warm, peaceful, and nostalgic. Good for winding down.

reggae.description: Jamaican-origin music with a distinctive off-beat rhythm and relaxed groove. Associated with positivity, sunshine, and laid-back enjoyment. Moderate energy, high warmth.

country.description: American roots music blending folk, blues, and pop. Often acoustic or semi-acoustic. Themes of home, nostalgia, heartbreak, and rural life.

focused.description: Music that aids concentration without demanding attention. Typically instrumental, low-distraction, moderate pace with minimal abrupt changes.

chill.description: Relaxed but not sleepy. Low stakes, comfortable, easy-listening. Good for unwinding after activity or background listening.

happy.description: Uplifting, bright music with positive emotional tone. High valence, often danceable, energetic. Good for celebrations, morning routines, or boosting mood.

energetic.description: High-intensity, driving music that sustains or boosts physical or mental output. Strong beat, fast tempo, high danceability.

intense.description: Emotionally or dynamically powerful music, not necessarily fast. Can feel cathartic, urgent, or overwhelming. High engagement required.

relaxed.description: Gentle, low-pressure music for leisure. Not quite as focused as 'chill' — more passive, comfortable, background-appropriate.

moody.description: Atmospheric, introspective music with emotional ambiguity. Not quite sad, not quite happy — contemplative, late-night, or cinematic.

romantic.description: Warm, intimate music associated with closeness, love, or longing. High valence, moderate energy, often acoustic or lush production.

peaceful.description: Calm, serene music without tension. Very low energy, high acousticness. Good for winding down, meditation, or quiet evenings.

sad.description: Music for processing difficult emotions — grief, heartbreak, loneliness. Low valence, slow tempo, often acoustic. Cathartic rather than escapist.

melancholy.description: A softer, more reflective sadness than acute grief. Bittersweet, often beautiful. Lower energy than 'sad' but more emotionally complex.

nostalgic.description: Music that evokes memories of the past — childhood, a place, a relationship. Warm and bittersweet, often with acoustic elements.

angry.description: Cathartic, aggressive music that channels frustration or rage. Very high energy, low valence. Used for emotional release rather than enjoyment.
